evals.prompt: What's the first step to making a new astrodb?
evals.expected_output: Explains that the first step is to create your own GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (via 'Use this template' on https://github.com/astrodbtoolkit/astrodb-template-db) and provide its URL, and asks for a name for the database — suggesting the user reuse that name for the new repo too. Does not ask for or mention a data file — that comes in a later step.

assertions.text: Instructs the user to create a new GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (e.g. 'Use this template' on astrodb-template-db) and to provide the new repo's URL
Asks the user for a database name
Suggests or encourages using the same name for the GitHub repo as for the database
Does not ask for or reference a data file, parsing, or ingestion (that is a later step)

evals.prompt: I made my repo from the template, it's at https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . Call the database BdSurvey and get it set up.
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb right away without asking where to put it, verifies structure, updates db_name to 'BdSurvey', asks for a README description, and prompts the user to update the LICENSE — putting their own name(s) on the copyright or choosing a different license, since none was given. In Step 6, offers to help write a directions document but does not automatically create an artifacts/ directory or write a directions.md file. Tells the user the cloned directory is their project directory. At the end — after setup is complete — notes the repo/database name mismatch (test-astrodb vs BdSurvey), asks if they want to rename the repo on GitHub, and offers to run 'git remote set-url origin' with the new URL once they do. Optionally offers to rename the local directory too. Does not raise the mismatch before or during cloning.

assertions.text: Clones the provided repo URL (https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb)
Does not ask the user where to clone the repo or what directory/working directory to use before cloning
Verifies the cloned repo has the template directory structure: a data/ directory, a database.toml file, and a schema.yaml file
Removes schema_erd.png from the cloned repo
Removes the .md files in docs/schema/ from the cloned repo
Updates database.toml so db_name is set to the user-provided name (BdSurvey)

Offers to help the user write a directions document, or explains it can be provided later as a path to downstream skills — does not automatically create an artifacts/ directory or write a directions.md file
After cloning and updating db_name, asks the user for a description of their database to use in the README
Updates README.md: replaces the template title (astrodb-template-db) with the database name (BdSurvey) and replaces the generic description with the user-provided one

Prompts the user to update the LICENSE copyright with their own name(s), or to choose a different license — since no name was given, does not silently leave it or claim to have set names the user never provided
In the wrap-up, tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for the next steps
After setup is complete, notes the repo/database name mismatch (test-astrodb vs BdSurvey) and asks if the user wants to rename the repo
Offers to run 'git remote set-url origin' with the new URL once the user renames the repo on GitHub
Does not raise the name mismatch before or during cloning — only raises it at the end
Does not ask for or handle a data file

evals.prompt: Set up a new astrodb database called NearbyGalaxies.
evals.expected_output: Notes the database name (NearbyGalaxies) and, because no repo URL was given, asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template ('Use this template') and provide its URL before cloning — suggesting the new repo be named NearbyGalaxies (or something close) to match the database. Does not invent a repo URL or fabricate a clone.

assertions.text: Asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template and provide its URL before cloning
Suggests naming the new GitHub repo NearbyGalaxies, or something close to it, to match the database name
Does not invent a repo URL or clone an arbitrary repository
Does not ask for or reference a data file

evals.prompt: I want to start a new astrodb for my study of M dwarf flares. Let's call the database MdwarfFlares.
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the database name (MdwarfFlares), suggests the user give their new GitHub repo the same (or a closely matching) name, and walks them through creating a repo from the astrodb-template and sending its URL — without inventing a URL, cloning anything, or asking about a data file yet.

assertions.text: Acknowledges or confirms the database will be named MdwarfFlares
Suggests or recommends naming the new GitHub repo the same as, or closely matching, MdwarfFlares
Explains how to create a repo from the astrodb-template (e.g. 'Use this template' on astrodb-template-db) and asks for its URL
Does not invent a repo URL or attempt to clone anything
Does not ask for or reference a data file

evals.prompt: Here's my repo for a new astrodb: https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . Can you set it up?
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb right away without asking where to put it or what working directory/folder to use, verifies the template structure, then asks for a database name (since none was given). When asking, suggests the user consider naming the database to match the repo (test-astrodb / TestAstrodb) or notes that if they pick a different name they may want to rename the repo. Sets db_name once the name is provided, asks for a README description, and prompts the user to update the LICENSE (put their own name(s) on the copyright, or choose a different license). In the final wrap-up, tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for next steps.

assertions.text: Does not ask the user where to clone the repo, what directory to use, or what their working directory should be before running git clone
Clones https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb
Asks the user for a database name, since none was provided in the prompt
When asking for the database name, suggests aligning it with the repo name (test-astrodb) or notes the option to rename the repo to match whatever name they choose
After cloning and updating db_name, asks the user for a description of their database to use in the README

Prompts the user to update the LICENSE copyright with their own name(s), or to choose a different license — since no name was given, does not silently leave it or claim to have set names the user never provided
In the wrap-up (after cloning), tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for subsequent steps

evals.prompt: I want to set up a new astrodb called BrownDwarfs. My repo is https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . It's a catalog of nearby brown dwarf systems with spectral types, photometry, and parallaxes — for studying the L/T transition.
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb without asking where to put it, verifies the template structure, updates database.toml so db_name is BrownDwarfs. For the README step, uses the description the user already provided ('a catalog of nearby brown dwarf systems with spectral types, photometry, and parallaxes — for studying the L/T transition') — or something very close — to update the README title and description without re-asking. Shows or confirms what was changed in the README. Prompts the user to update the LICENSE — their own name(s) on the copyright, or a different license — since no name was given in the prompt. In the wrap-up, tells the user the repo directory is their project directory and points to the next step.

assertions.text: Clones https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb without asking where to put it
Updates database.toml so db_name is set to BrownDwarfs
Updates README.md: replaces the template title (astrodb-template-db) with BrownDwarfs
Updates README.md description to reflect the brown dwarf / L-T transition science the user described — does not leave the generic 'A template schema for astronomical databases' text

Prompts the user to update the LICENSE copyright with their own name(s), or to choose a different license — since no name was given, does not silently leave it or claim to have set names the user never provided
Does not ask the user to re-describe their database — the description was already given in the prompt
In the wrap-up, tells the user the cloned repo directory is now their project directory for subsequent steps

evals.prompt: Set up a new astrodb called CoolStars from my repo https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . Put my name, Jane Astronomer, on it.
evals.expected_output: Clones the repo, verifies structure, sets db_name to CoolStars, asks for a README description, and — because the user gave a name — updates the LICENSE copyright line so it credits Jane Astronomer (added alongside the existing template authors, or replacing them). Uses the Edit tool to change only the copyright line, keeping the rest of the BSD 3-Clause text intact; does not silently skip the license or leave the template authors as the only names.

assertions.text: Updates database.toml so db_name is set to CoolStars
Updates the LICENSE copyright line to credit the user-provided name (Jane Astronomer), rather than leaving the template authors as the only names
Edits only the copyright line and keeps the license as BSD 3-Clause (no other license was requested) — does not rewrite or corrupt the rest of the LICENSE text
Does not ask for or handle a data file

evals.prompt: I'm setting up an astrodb called OpenCatalog at https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . Please put it under the MIT license, under my name Sam Researcher.
evals.expected_output: Clones the repo, verifies structure, sets db_name to OpenCatalog, asks for a README description, and replaces the LICENSE file with the standard MIT License text — filling in the current year and 'Sam Researcher' in the copyright line. Does not leave a placeholder year or name, and does not leave the original BSD 3-Clause text in place.

assertions.text: Updates database.toml so db_name is set to OpenCatalog
Replaces the LICENSE contents with standard MIT License text, not the original BSD 3-Clause license
The new MIT LICENSE has the user's name (Sam Researcher) and a real year filled into the copyright line — no leftover placeholder like <year>, [year], or [name]
Does not ask for or handle a data file

evals.prompt: Set up a new astrodb called StreamDB from https://github.com/kelle/test-astrodb . I already have a directions doc at /Users/me/notes/stream_directions.md .
evals.expected_output: Clones the repo, verifies structure, sets db_name to StreamDB. In Step 6 (directions document), reads /Users/me/notes/stream_directions.md immediately — since the user already provided a path — and acknowledges it without re-asking whether to write one. Does not create an artifacts/ directory or copy the file anywhere automatically. Continues to README and LICENSE steps and wraps up.

assertions.text: Reads the directions document from the user-provided path (/Users/me/notes/stream_directions.md) rather than asking the user to write a new one
Does not ask the user whether they want to write a directions document — they already supplied one
Updates database.toml so db_name is set to StreamDB
Does not ask for or handle a data file
